description:
This illustration depicts the differentiation between Permanent Scatterers (PS) and Distributed Scatterers (DS) in radar interferometry, alongside the logos of Copernicus, the European Commission, and the European Environment Agency (EEA).

The main visual shows a satellite (resembling a Sentinel satellite) emitting radar signals towards a conceptual grid-based landscape. The legend defines the classification of ground features:
*   Red represents PS (Permanent Scatterers).
*   Blue represents DS (Distributed Scatterers).
*   Orange represents No Data.

On the ground grid, various land cover types are visible: forests (evergreen trees), agricultural fields, and electricity pylons. The red PS cells are consistently associated with robust, man-made structures such as the electricity pylons. The blue DS cells are associated with natural, homogeneous surfaces like agricultural fields and some forested areas. The orange "No Data" cells are also associated with agricultural fields.

An embedded chart, titled "Received Signal," shows the typical signal characteristics for PS and DS. The Y-axis is labelled "Signal," and the X-axis is labelled "Range." The PS signal (red line/bars) is characterized by high-amplitude, sharp, and distinct peaks. The DS signal (blue line/bars) is characterized by lower-amplitude, broader, and more numerous peaks, indicating a less coherent reflection.
